growth_records.cite: Laursen MF et al. Bifidobacterium species associated with breastfeeding produce aromatic lactic acids in the infant gut. Nature Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41564-021-00970-4. [PMC8556157]
growth_records.snippet: The cultures were sampled (500 ul) every hour for OD600nm measurements and the culture supernatants ... from early (13 h) stationary phase were analysed by UPLC-MS for aromatic amino metabolites and by GC-MS for lactate. ... Curves show mea
growth_records.notes: This is NOT a genome-scale-model / 13C-MFA paper; it is a Bifidobacterium physiology/enzymology study. The only extractable-in-text quantitative metabolic data are in-vitro ENZYME kinetics for the type-4 LDH/ALDH (kcat/K0.5: indolepyruvate 194 s-1 mM-1, 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate 16 s-1 mM-1, phenylpyruvate 11 s-1 mM-1; Fig. 3) - these are purified-enzyme parameters, NOT whole-cell growth-condition uptake/secretion fluxes, so not entered as rates. The whole-cell growth physiology that IS reported (specific growth / doubling times in Fig. 2d; secreted ILA, PLA, 4-OH-PLA and lactate amounts in Fig. 2e; production by ~14 type strains on glucose vs HMO in Fig. 2a) has its NUMERIC values only in figure images / Source data files, which are not present in the PMC XML fulltext. Therefore all rate fields are null (no numbers invented, per instruction). One representative record emitted for the genetically defined B. longum ssp. longum 105-A WT batch condition to preserve organism+medium+condition and the qualitative measured secretion. Medium = MRSc = MRS broth + 2% (w/v) glucose + 0.05% (w/v) L-cysteine (base MRS component list not itemised in paper). Conditions: 37 C, anaerobic, batch, shaking; supernatant at 13 h early stationary phase. No pH, no growth rate value, no yields extractable from text. Growth was not impaired by type-4 ldh disruption (Fig. 2d).

growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Bifidobacterium longum YL2 (Actinobacteria), Max OD 1.01, Mean μ ± SD 0.91 ± 0.04.
growth_records.notes: μ=0.91 h^-1 (±0.04) reported. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.91 = 0.762 h. Max OD600 = 1.01. Only Actinobacteria member that grew.

up.method: not quantified (2% glucose provided in medium; consumption rate not reported)

growth_records.cite: Plenge TG et al. Aromatic lactic acid production by Bifidobacterium longum subsp. infantis is determined by cell density, substrate availability, and pH in vitro. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1128/aem.02511-25. [PMC13274450]
growth_records.snippet: PLA was the most abundant ALA in the non-supplemented culture (208 ± 29 µM), followed by OH-PLA (105 ± 13 µM) and ILA (50 ± 8 µM). ... All cultures reached an OD600nm between 2.6 and 3.6.
growth_records.notes: Batch control (no AAA supplementation) in MRS-cys broth, anaerobic chamber, 37C, 48 h endpoint. Aromatic lactic acids (ALAs) are secreted products; concentrations are 48 h endpoint values (µM), not volumetric or specific rates (no biomass in gDW, no time-resolved rate). No growth rate reported for batch cultures (specific growth rate μ only reported for chemostat exponential phase, and only in Fig 2C image). Final OD600 2.6-3.6. Glucose 2% (=20 g/L) and 0.05% cysteine added after autoclaving. ILA/OH-PLA/PLA derived from Trp/Tyr/Phe respectively via aromatic lactate dehydrogenase Aldh.

up.method: not reported (glucose 20 g/L in feed; uptake rate not quantified)

sec.units: µM (steady-state supernatant; values in Fig 3A image, similar to pH 5.5)
sec.method: LC-MS/MS; higher ALA at pH 5.5/6.5 than pH 4.5

growth_records.cite: Plenge TG et al. Aromatic lactic acid production by Bifidobacterium longum subsp. infantis is determined by cell density, substrate availability, and pH in vitro. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1128/aem.02511-25. [PMC13274450]
growth_records.snippet: B. infantis was grown in chemostat cultures at a dilution rate of 0.06 h−1 ... 37°C, the pH was controlled at constant levels (4.5, 5.5, and 6.5) ... stirring speed was 100 rpm. ... cell numbers were kept stable around ≈10^9 CFU mL−1 for pH
growth_records.notes: Chemostat pH 6.5. Steady state reached (OD600 stabilized after 48 h; CFU stable ≈10^9/mL 48-96 h). At chemostat steady state μ = D, so growth_rate_per_h = 0.06 h⁻¹ (dilution rate stated). Doubling time = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.06 = 11.55 h. Specific growth rate from batch exponential phase reported only in Fig 2C image (numeric μ/DT not in text). ALA secretion concentrations (µM) are in Fig 3A image (not text-extractable numbers); no per-cell/gDW secretion rate. Feed = 500 mL MRS broth + 0.5 g/L cysteine-HCl + 20 g/L glucose.
